title: Raw
label: Black Book Records

structure.description: Kick + hats + riser kick in, no bass
Drone synth E4 + clock loop, no kick
Groove loop + shaker + snare roll build
Full arrangement — kick, bass, lead, groove loop. Reese enters bar 31.
Identical to Drop 1. Reese enters bar 39.
Drone + groove loop + shaker + snare roll
Full drop, same structure. Reese bar 55.
Bass variation — Eb3 enters, adds harmonic colour
Drone synth only — maximum tension, no percussion
Siren + riser + lazer down + snare roll escalation
Final drop cycle begins. Reese bar 91.

tracks.name: Tops
tracks.role: Hi-hat pattern — open hat + closed hat

tracks.name: Bass
tracks.role: Main bass riff — E3 dominant with F3 tension

tracks.name: Pluck Bass
tracks.role: Short pluck bass accents on off-bars

tracks.name: Drone Synth
tracks.role: Sustained E4 every 4 beats through breakdowns

tracks.name: Stab
tracks.role: C4 chord stab on transitions (4 hits, velocity 62)

tracks.name: Vocal
tracks.role: Main vocal sample — runs almost entire track (bar 4.8–111)

tracks.name: Groove Loop
tracks.role: Percussive groove loop, layers into drops

tracks.name: Shaker Groove
tracks.role: Shaker pattern, pairs with groove loop

tracks.name: White Noise SC
tracks.role: Sidechained white noise — adds air + pump

tracks.name: Crash R
tracks.role: Crash cymbal on drop transitions (bar 32, 40, etc.)

tracks.name: Pre Fill
tracks.role: Short fill samples before each drop

tracks.name: Lazer
tracks.role: Short laser hit (used in rapid sequence before crash)

open_hat.pattern: Every off-beat — 0.5, 1.5, 2.5, 3.5 per bar (8th note off-beats)

closed_hat.pattern: 16th note positions with heavily humanised velocity

bass_pattern.description: E3 is the dominant bass note throughout. F3 arrives at beat 7 of the 2-bar phrase (one semitone above root) creating phrygian tension. Eb4 hits on beat 3 of each bar as an accent — this is the minor 7th, adds harmonic colour. E5 is a brief high accent at bar position 11.5.
bass_pattern.theory_note: The E→F movement is phrygian — the flat 2nd (b2) creates an aggressive, dark tension before the phrase resets. This is a hallmark of hard UK garage and bassline.

reese_pattern.description: F3 held for 3.5 beats, then resolves down to E3 for 2.5 beats. Appears in the final 2 bars of each 8-bar drop section for peak intensity.
reese_pattern.theory_note: The Reese is a detuned sawtooth bass (Serum). The F→E movement mirrors the main bass tension — it's the same phrygian b2→root resolution but held longer, making the drop feel like it's resolving and collapsing at the same time.

lead_pattern.description: Single-note E5 melodic phrase spanning 6 bars, with syncopated rhythm. Enters just before the drop. Simple but effective — everything is in the bass, the lead just reinforces the root.

key_techniques: 4-on-the-floor kick with humanised hi-hat velocities — feels alive, not robotic
Phrygian b2 tension: E→F semitone in bass creates aggression without a chord progression
Reese bass (Serum detuned sawtooth) used only in last 2 bars of each drop — restraint amplifies impact
Drone synth sustains root (E4) through breakdowns — holds harmonic context while percussion drops
Sidechained white noise (White Noise SC) adds pumping air to the drops
Lazer hits in rapid sequence (0.2 bar intervals) immediately before crash cymbal — tension build trick
No chord loop — entire harmonic language is in the single bass note and its phrygian neighbour F
theory_notes: MPH Raw is harmonically minimalist — there is no chord progression. The entire track is built on a single pedal note (E) with the phrygian b2 (F) providing all the tension. Analytically: think of the E→F movement as a 1-semitone oscillation around the root, like a pendulum. The energy comes entirely from rhythm, timbre (the Reese), and arrangement — not harmony. This makes it a perfect first track to recreate because you don't need to understand chords at all. You need to understand groove, tension, and when to withhold elements.

elements_to_transplant: The phrygian b2 bass movement (any key: root→semitone above)
The 2-bar Reese resolution at drop peak
